rulings.Case Title: SCOTT MICHAEL MCDONALD VS. MERRILL LYNCH PIERCE, FENNER & SMITH INCORPORATED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petitioner'S Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award; Notice Of Motion And Motion To Confirm Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 13, 2019, Line 1, PETITIONER SCOTT MCDONALD's Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award. Grant. No opposition filed. The Court will enter the proposed Order and Judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for petitioner is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: SHELDON T FONG VS. MICHAEL VRIHEAS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANT CECILIA VRIHEAS' Motion For An Order Compelling Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted. Plaintiff shall serve code compliant responses to Special Interrogatories within 10 days. Sanctions are awarded against defendant in the amount of $335. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DEVLOPERS SURETY AND INDEMNITY COMPANY VS. RLL, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Developers Surety And Indemnity Company For Amendment Of Judgment To Correct Clerical Error
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 13, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF DEVELOPERS SURETY AND INDEMNITY COMPANY's Motion Of Developers Surety And Indemnity Company For Amendment Of Judgment To Correct Clerical Error. Plaintiff Developers Surety and Indemnity Company's motion for amendment of judgment to correct clerical error is granted. The judgment entered by this Court on August 24, 2018 shall be corrected to reflect the correct spelling of the name of defaulting defendant Brenda Alessandria. Defendant's "motion to set aside default judgment" is off calendar because it lacks a separate notice of motion and motion (CRC 3.1110, 3.1112) and it was not timely filed and served at least 16 court days prior to the hearing (Code Civ. Proc. § 1005). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JESSICA BAYZE VS. QIANG WU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Further Respond To Defendant Qtang Wus Special Interrogatories (Set One) And Requests For Production Of Documents (Set One) And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Her Attorneys
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 13, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANTS QIANG WU, YOUNGWEN TONG, TONG WU'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Further Respond To Defendant Qtang Wus Special Interrogatories (Set One) And Requests For Production Of Documents (Set One) And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Her Attorneys Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted in part and denied in part. Within ten days, Plaintiff shall amend her responses to either Special Interrogatory No. 53 and/or Request for Production No. 36 in order to provide a consistent response as to whether or not there was fire damage to the premises. A further response to Special Interrogatory is not required since the Interrogatory is irrelevant to determining or estimating attorneys' fees. No sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL VS. FIDELIS CYBERSECURITY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 13, 2019, Line 11, PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's MOTION TO STRIKE GENERAL DENIAL. Grant. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the City is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing.=(302/EPS)
